## Consolidator Types

Consolidating Data > Consolidator Types > Time Period Consolidators

# Consolidator Types

## Time Period Consolidators

### Introduction

Time period consolidators aggregate data based on a period of time like a number of seconds, minutes, or days. If a time period consolidator aggregates data over multiple days, the multi-day aggregation cycle starts and repeats based on the time stamp of the first data point you use to update the consolidator.

### Consolidate Trade Bars

TradeBar consolidators aggregate TradeBar objects into TradeBar objects of the same size or larger. Follow these steps to create and manage a TradeBar consolidator based on a period of time:

- 1. Create the consolidator.


To set the time period for the consolidator, you can use either a timedelta or Resolution object. The consolidator time period must be greater than or equal to the resolution of the security subscription. For instance, you can aggregate minute bars into 10-minute bars, but you can't aggregate hour bars into 10-minute bars.

timedelta Periods

![image 211](<Quantconnect-Writing-Algorithms-Python_part2_images/imageFile211.png>)

PY

self._consolidator = TradeBarConsolidator(timedelta(days=1)) # Aliases: # self._consolidator = self.create_consolidator(timedelta(days=1), TradeBar) # self._consolidator = self.resolve_consolidator(self._symbol, timedelta(days=1))

The time period is relative to the exchange time zone , not the algorithm time zone . If you consolidate Crypto data into daily bars, the event handler receives the consolidated bars at midnight 12:00 AM Coordinated Universal Time (UTC), regardless of the algorithm time zone.

Use the start_time parameter of the TradeBarConsolidator type constructor to define a custom start time.

![image 212](<Quantconnect-Writing-Algorithms-Python_part2_images/imageFile212.png>)

PY

self._consolidator = TradeBarConsolidator(timedelta(days=1), start_time=timedelta(hours=9, minutes=30))

Resolution Periods

The Resolution enumeration has the following members:

![image 213](<Quantconnect-Writing-Algorithms-Python_part2_images/imageFile213.png>)

PY

self._consolidator = TradeBarConsolidator.from_resolution(Resolution.DAILY) # Alias: # self._consolidator = self.resolve_consolidator(self._symbol, Resolution.DAILY)

If the security subscription in your algorithm provides TradeBar and QuoteBar data, resolve_consolidator returns a TradeBarConsolidator .

If you consolidate on an hourly basis, the consolidator ends at the top of the hour, not every hour after the market open. For US Equities, that's 10 AM Eastern Time (ET), not 10:30 AM ET. To change this behavior, set the start_time parameter of the TradeBarConsolidator type constructor to timedelta(hours=9, minutes=30)

- 2. Add an event handler to the consolidator.

LEAN passes consolidated bars to the consolidator event handler in your algorithm. The most common error when creating consolidators is to put parenthesis () at the end of your method name when setting the event handler of the consolidator. If you use parenthesis, the method executes and the result is passed as the event handler instead of the method itself. Remember to pass the name of your method to the event system. Specifically, it should be self._consolidation_handler , not self._consolidation_handler() .

- 3. Define the consolidation handler.

The consolidation event handler receives bars when the consolidated bar closes based on the exchange time zone . If you subscribe to minute resolution data for Bitcoin and create an hourly consolidator, you receive consolidated bars at the top of each hour. However, if you subscribe to minute resolution data for the regular trading hours of US Equities and create a daily consolidator, you receive consolidated bars at market close or midnight, depending on your daily_precise_end_time setting .

- 4. Update the consolidator.

You can automatically or manually update the consolidator.

Automatic Updates

To automatically update a consolidator with data from the security subscription, call the add_consolidator method of the Subscription Manager.

Manual Updates

Manual updates let you control when the consolidator updates and what data you use to update it. If you need to warm up a consolidator with data outside of the warm-up period , you can manually update the consolidator. To manually update a consolidator, call its update method with a TradeBar object. You can update the consolidator with data from the Slice object in the on_data method or with data from a history request .

- 5. If you create consolidators for securities in a dynamic universe and register them for automatic updates, remove the consolidator when the security leaves the universe.


![image 214](<Quantconnect-Writing-Algorithms-Python_part2_images/imageFile214.png>)

PY

self._consolidator.data_consolidated += self._consolidation_handler

![image 215](<Quantconnect-Writing-Algorithms-Python_part2_images/imageFile215.png>)

PY

def _consolidation_handler(self, sender: object, consolidated_bar: TradeBar) -> None: pass

![image 216](<Quantconnect-Writing-Algorithms-Python_part2_images/imageFile216.png>)

PY

self.subscription_manager.add_consolidator(self._symbol, self._consolidator)

![image 217](<Quantconnect-Writing-Algorithms-Python_part2_images/imageFile217.png>)

PY

- # Example 1: Update the consolidator with data from the Slice object def on_data(self, slice: Slice) -> None:

trade_bar = slice.bars[self._symbol] self._consolidator.update(trade_bar)

- # Example 2: Update the consolidator with data from a history request history = self.history[TradeBar](self._symbol, 30, Resolution.MINUTE) for trade_bar in history:


self._consolidator.update(trade_bar)

![image 218](<Quantconnect-Writing-Algorithms-Python_part2_images/imageFile218.png>)

PY

self.subscription_manager.remove_consolidator(self._symbol, self._consolidator)

If you have a dynamic universe and don't remove consolidators, they compound internally, causing your algorithm to slow down and eventually die once it runs out of RAM. For an example of removing consolidators from universe subscriptions, see the GasAndCrudeOilEnergyCorrelationAlpha in the LEAN GitHub repository.

You can also use the Consolidate helper method to create period consolidators and register them for automatic updates. With just one line of code,

#### you can create data in any time period based on a timedelta or Resolution object:

timedelta Periods

![image 219](<Quantconnect-Writing-Algorithms-Python_part2_images/imageFile219.png>)

PY

self._consolidator = self.consolidate(self._symbol, timedelta(days=1), self._consolidation_handler)

Resolution Periods

![image 220](<Quantconnect-Writing-Algorithms-Python_part2_images/imageFile220.png>)

PY

self._consolidator = self.consolidate(self._symbol, Resolution.DAILY, self._consolidation_handler)

If you use the consolidate helper method, the consolidation handler doesn't receive an object argument.

![image 221](<Quantconnect-Writing-Algorithms-Python_part2_images/imageFile221.png>)

PY

def _consolidation_handler(self, consolidated_bar: TradeBar) -> None: pass

### Consolidate Quote Bars

QuoteBar consolidators aggregate QuoteBar objects into QuoteBar objects of the same size or larger. Follow these steps to create and manage a QuoteBar consolidator based on a period of time:

- 1. Create the consolidator.

To set the time period for the consolidator, you can use either a timedelta or Resolution object. The consolidator time period must be greater than or equal to the resolution of the security subscription. For instance, you can aggregate minute bars into 10-minute bars, but you can't aggregate hour bars into 10-minute bars.

timedelta Periods

The time period is relative to the exchange time zone , not the algorithm time zone . If you consolidate Crypto data into daily bars, the event handler receives the consolidated bars at midnight 12:00 AM Coordinated Universal Time (UTC), regardless of the algorithm time zone.

Use the start_time parameter of the QuoteBarConsolidator type constructor to define a custom start time.

Resolution Periods

The Resolution enumeration has the following members:

If the security subscription in your algorithm provides TradeBar and QuoteBar data, resolve_consolidator returns a TradeBarConsolidator .

If you consolidate on an hourly basis, the consolidator ends at the top of the hour, not every hour after the market open. For US Equities, that's 10 AM Eastern Time (ET), not 10:30 AM ET. To change this behavior, set the start_time parameter of the QuoteBarConsolidator type constructor to timedelta(hours=9, minutes=30)

- 2. Add an event handler to the consolidator.


![image 222](<Quantconnect-Writing-Algorithms-Python_part2_images/imageFile222.png>)

PY

self._consolidator = QuoteBarConsolidator(timedelta(days=1)) # Aliases: # self._consolidator = self.create_consolidator(timedelta(days=1), QuoteBar) # self._consolidator = self.resolve_consolidator(self._symbol, timedelta(days=1))

![image 223](<Quantconnect-Writing-Algorithms-Python_part2_images/imageFile223.png>)

PY

self._consolidator = QuoteBarConsolidator(timedelta(days=1), start_time=timedelta(hours=9, minutes=30))

![image 224](<Quantconnect-Writing-Algorithms-Python_part2_images/imageFile224.png>)

PY

self._consolidator = self.resolve_consolidator(self._symbol, Resolution.DAILY)

![image 225](<Quantconnect-Writing-Algorithms-Python_part2_images/imageFile225.png>)

PY

self._consolidator.data_consolidated += self._consolidation_handler

LEAN passes consolidated bars to the consolidator event handler in your algorithm. The most common error when creating consolidators is to put parenthesis () at the end of your method name when setting the event handler of the consolidator. If you use parenthesis, the method executes and the result is passed as the event handler instead of the method itself. Remember to pass the name of your method to the event system. Specifically, it should be self._consolidation_handler , not self._consolidation_handler() .

- 3. Define the consolidation handler.

The consolidation event handler receives bars when the consolidated bar closes based on the exchange time zone . If you subscribe to minute resolution data for Bitcoin and create an hourly consolidator, you receive consolidated bars at the top of each hour. However, if you subscribe to minute resolution data for the regular trading hours of US Equities and create a daily consolidator, you receive consolidated bars at market close or midnight, depending on your daily_precise_end_time setting .

- 4. Update the consolidator.

You can automatically or manually update the consolidator.

Automatic Updates

To automatically update a consolidator with data from the security subscription, call the add_consolidator method of the Subscription Manager.

Manual Updates

Manual updates let you control when the consolidator updates and what data you use to update it. If you need to warm up a consolidator with data outside of the warm-up period , you can manually update the consolidator. To manually update a consolidator, call its update method with a QuoteBar object. You can update the consolidator with data from the Slice object in the on_data method or with data from a history request .

- 5. If you create consolidators for securities in a dynamic universe and register them for automatic updates, remove the consolidator when the security leaves the universe.


![image 226](<Quantconnect-Writing-Algorithms-Python_part2_images/imageFile226.png>)

PY

def _consolidation_handler(self, sender: object, consolidated_bar: QuoteBar) -> None: pass

![image 227](<Quantconnect-Writing-Algorithms-Python_part2_images/imageFile227.png>)

PY

self.subscription_manager.add_consolidator(self._symbol, self._consolidator)

![image 228](<Quantconnect-Writing-Algorithms-Python_part2_images/imageFile228.png>)

PY

# Example 1: Update the consolidator with data from the Slice object def on_data(self, slice: Slice) -> None:

quote_bar = slice.quote_bars[self._symbol] self._consolidator.update(quote_bar)

# Example 2: Update the consolidator with data from a history request history = self.history[QuoteBar](self._symbol, 30, Resolution.MINUTE) for quote_bar in history:

self._consolidator.update(quote_bar)

![image 229](<Quantconnect-Writing-Algorithms-Python_part2_images/imageFile229.png>)

PY

self.subscription_manager.remove_consolidator(self._symbol, self._consolidator)

If you have a dynamic universe and don't remove consolidators, they compound internally, causing your algorithm to slow down and eventually die once it runs out of RAM. For an example of removing consolidators from universe subscriptions, see the GasAndCrudeOilEnergyCorrelationAlpha in the LEAN GitHub repository.

You can also use the Consolidate helper method to create period consolidators and register them for automatic updates. With just one line of code, you can create data in any time period based on a timedelta or Resolution object:

timedelta Periods

![image 230](<Quantconnect-Writing-Algorithms-Python_part2_images/imageFile230.png>)

PY

self._consolidator = self.consolidate(self._symbol, timedelta(days=1), TickType.Quote, self._consolidation_handler)

Resolution Periods

![image 231](<Quantconnect-Writing-Algorithms-Python_part2_images/imageFile231.png>)

PY

self._consolidator = self.consolidate(self._symbol, Resolution.DAILY, TickType.Quote, self._consolidation_handler)

If you use the consolidate helper method, the consolidation handler doesn't receive an object argument.

![image 232](<Quantconnect-Writing-Algorithms-Python_part2_images/imageFile232.png>)

PY

def _consolidation_handler(self, consolidated_bar: QuoteBar) -> None: pass

### Consolidate Trade Ticks

Tick consolidators aggregate Tick objects into TradeBar objects. Follow these steps to create and manage a Tick consolidator based on a period of time:

- 1. Create the consolidator.

To set the time period for the consolidator, you can use either a timedelta or Resolution object. The consolidator time period must be greater than or equal to the resolution of the security subscription. For instance, you can aggregate minute bars into 10-minute bars, but you can't aggregate hour bars into 10-minute bars.

timedelta Periods

The time period is relative to the exchange time zone , not the algorithm time zone . If you consolidate Crypto data into daily bars, the event handler receives the consolidated bars at midnight 12:00 AM Coordinated Universal Time (UTC), regardless of the algorithm time zone.

Use the start_time parameter of the TickConsolidator type constructor to define a custom start time.

Resolution Periods

The Resolution enumeration has the following members:

If you consolidate on an hourly basis, the consolidator ends at the top of the hour, not every hour after the market open. For US Equities, that's 10 AM Eastern Time (ET), not 10:30 AM ET. To change this behavior, set the start_time parameter of the TickConsolidator type constructor to timedelta(hours=9, minutes=30)

- 2. Add an event handler to the consolidator.


![image 233](<Quantconnect-Writing-Algorithms-Python_part2_images/imageFile233.png>)

PY

self._consolidator = TickConsolidator(timedelta(milliseconds=100)) # Aliases: # self._consolidator = self.create_consolidator(timedelta(milliseconds=100), Tick) # self._consolidator = self.resolve_consolidator(self._symbol, timedelta(milliseconds=100))

![image 234](<Quantconnect-Writing-Algorithms-Python_part2_images/imageFile234.png>)

PY

self._consolidator = TickConsolidator(timedelta(milliseconds=100), start_time=timedelta(hours=9, minutes=30))

![image 235](<Quantconnect-Writing-Algorithms-Python_part2_images/imageFile235.png>)

PY

self._consolidator = self.resolve_consolidator(self._symbol, Resolution.SECOND)

![image 236](<Quantconnect-Writing-Algorithms-Python_part2_images/imageFile236.png>)

PY

self._consolidator.data_consolidated += self._consolidation_handler

LEAN passes consolidated bars to the consolidator event handler in your algorithm. The most common error when creating consolidators is to put parenthesis () at the end of your method name when setting the event handler of the consolidator. If you use parenthesis, the method executes and the result is passed as the event handler instead of the method itself. Remember to pass the name of your method to the event system. Specifically, it should be self._consolidation_handler , not self._consolidation_handler() .

- 3. Define the consolidation handler.

The consolidation event handler receives bars when the consolidated bar closes based on the exchange time zone . If you subscribe to tick resolution data for Bitcoin and create an hourly consolidator, you receive consolidated bars at the top of each hour. However, if you subscribe to tick resolution data for the regular trading hours of US Equities and create a daily consolidator, you receive consolidated bars at market close or midnight, depending on your daily_precise_end_time setting .

- 4. Update the consolidator.

You can automatically or manually update the consolidator.

Automatic Updates

To automatically update a consolidator with data from the security subscription, call the add_consolidator method of the Subscription Manager.

Manual Updates

Manual updates let you control when the consolidator updates and what data you use to update it. If you need to warm up a consolidator with data outside of the warm-up period , you can manually update the consolidator. To manually update a consolidator, call its update method with a Tick object. You can update the consolidator with data from the Slice object in the on_data method or with data from a history request .

- 5. If you create consolidators for securities in a dynamic universe and register them for automatic updates, remove the consolidator when the security leaves the universe.


![image 237](<Quantconnect-Writing-Algorithms-Python_part2_images/imageFile237.png>)

PY

def _consolidation_handler(self, sender: object, consolidated_bar: TradeBar) -> None: pass

![image 238](<Quantconnect-Writing-Algorithms-Python_part2_images/imageFile238.png>)

PY

self.subscription_manager.add_consolidator(self._symbol, self._consolidator)

![image 239](<Quantconnect-Writing-Algorithms-Python_part2_images/imageFile239.png>)

PY

- # Example 1: Update the consolidator with data from the Slice object def on_data(self, slice: Slice) -> None:

ticks = slice.ticks[self._symbol] for tick in ticks:

self._consolidator.update(tick)

- # Example 2: Update the consolidator with data from a history request ticks = self.history[Tick](self._symbol, timedelta(minutes=3), Resolution.TICK) for tick in ticks:


self._consolidator.update(tick)

![image 240](<Quantconnect-Writing-Algorithms-Python_part2_images/imageFile240.png>)

PY

self.subscription_manager.remove_consolidator(self._symbol, self._consolidator)

If you have a dynamic universe and don't remove consolidators, they compound internally, causing your algorithm to slow down and eventually die once it runs out of RAM. For an example of removing consolidators from universe subscriptions, see the GasAndCrudeOilEnergyCorrelationAlpha in the LEAN GitHub repository.

You can also use the Consolidate helper method to create period consolidators and register them for automatic updates. With just one line of code, you can create data in any time period based on a timedelta or Resolution object:

timedelta Periods

![image 241](<Quantconnect-Writing-Algorithms-Python_part2_images/imageFile241.png>)

PY

self._consolidator = self.consolidate(self._symbol, timedelta(milliseconds=100), self._consolidation_handler)

Resolution Periods

![image 242](<Quantconnect-Writing-Algorithms-Python_part2_images/imageFile242.png>)

PY

self._consolidator = self.consolidate(self._symbol, Resolution.SECOND, self._consolidation_handler)

If you use the consolidate helper method, the consolidation handler doesn't receive an object argument.

![image 243](<Quantconnect-Writing-Algorithms-Python_part2_images/imageFile243.png>)

PY

def _consolidation_handler(self, consolidated_bar: TradeBar) -> None: pass

### Consolidate Quote Ticks

Tick quote bar consolidators aggregate Tick objects that represent quotes into QuoteBar objects. Follow these steps to create and manage a Tick quote bar consolidator based on a period of time:

- 1. Create the consolidator.

To set the time period for the consolidator, you can use either a timedelta or Resolution object. The consolidator time period must be greater than or equal to the resolution of the security subscription. For instance, you can aggregate minute bars into 10-minute bars, but you can't aggregate hour bars into 10-minute bars.

timedelta Periods

The time period is relative to the exchange time zone , not the algorithm time zone . If you consolidate Crypto data into daily bars, the event handler receives the consolidated bars at midnight 12:00 AM Coordinated Universal Time (UTC), regardless of the algorithm time zone.

Use the start_time parameter of the TickQuoteBarConsolidator type constructor to define a custom start time.

Resolution Periods

The Resolution enumeration has the following members:

If you consolidate on an hourly basis, the consolidator ends at the top of the hour, not every hour after the market open. For US Equities, that's 10 AM Eastern Time (ET), not 10:30 AM ET. To change this behavior, set the start_time parameter of the TickQuoteBarConsolidator type constructor to timedelta(hours=9, minutes=30)

- 2. Add an event handler to the consolidator.


![image 244](<Quantconnect-Writing-Algorithms-Python_part2_images/imageFile244.png>)

PY

self._consolidator = TickQuoteBarConsolidator(timedelta(milliseconds=100)) # Aliases: # self._consolidator = self.create_consolidator(timedelta(milliseconds=100), Tick, TickType.QUOTE) # self._consolidator = self.resolve_consolidator(self._symbol, timedelta(milliseconds=100))

![image 245](<Quantconnect-Writing-Algorithms-Python_part2_images/imageFile245.png>)

![image 247](<Quantconnect-Writing-Algorithms-Python_part2_images/imageFile247.png>)

LEAN passes consolidated bars to the consolidator event handler in your algorithm. The most common error when creating consolidators is to put parenthesis () at the end of your method name when setting the event handler of the consolidator. If you use parenthesis, the method

executes and the result is passed as the event handler instead of the method itself. Remember to pass the name of your method to the event system. Specifically, it should be self._consolidation_handler , not self._consolidation_handler() .

- 3. Define the consolidation handler.

The consolidation event handler receives bars when the consolidated bar closes based on the exchange time zone . If you subscribe to tick resolution data for Bitcoin and create an hourly consolidator, you receive consolidated bars at the top of each hour. However, if you subscribe to tick resolution data for the regular trading hours of US Equities and create a daily consolidator, you receive consolidated bars at market close or midnight, depending on your daily_precise_end_time setting .

- 4. Update the consolidator.

You can automatically or manually update the consolidator.

Automatic Updates

To automatically update a consolidator with data from the security subscription, call the add_consolidator method of the Subscription Manager.

Manual Updates

Manual updates let you control when the consolidator updates and what data you use to update it. If you need to warm up a consolidator with data outside of the warm-up period , you can manually update the consolidator. To manually update a consolidator, call its update method with a Tick object. You can update the consolidator with data from the Slice object in the on_data method or with data from a history request .

- 5. If you create consolidators for securities in a dynamic universe and register them for automatic updates, remove the consolidator when the security leaves the universe.

### Consolidate Other Data

<dataType> consolidators aggregate various types of data objects into <dataType> objects of the same size or larger. If you consolidate custom data or alternative datasets, check the definition of the data class to see its data type. Follow these steps to create and manage a <dataType> consolidator based on a period of time:

- 1. Create the consolidator.

To set the time period for the consolidator, you can use either a timedelta or Resolution object. The consolidator time period must be greater than or equal to the resolution of the security subscription. For instance, you can aggregate minute bars into 10-minute bars, but you can't aggregate hour bars into 10-minute bars.

timedelta Periods

The time period is relative to the exchange time zone , not the algorithm time zone . If you consolidate Crypto data into daily bars, the event handler receives the consolidated bars at midnight 12:00 AM Coordinated Universal Time (UTC), regardless of the algorithm time zone.

Use the start_time parameter of the DynamicDataConsolidator type constructor to define a custom start time.

Resolution Periods

The Resolution enumeration has the following members:

If you consolidate on an hourly basis, the consolidator ends at the top of the hour, not every hour after the market open. For US Equities, that's 10 AM Eastern Time (ET), not 10:30 AM ET. To change this behavior, set the start_time parameter of the DynamicDataConsolidator type constructor to timedelta(hours=9, minutes=30)

- 2. Add an event handler to the consolidator.


![image 255](<Quantconnect-Writing-Algorithms-Python_part2_images/imageFile255.png>)

PY

self._consolidator = DynamicDataConsolidator(timedelta(days=1)) # Aliases: # self._consolidator = self.create_consolidator(timedelta(days=1), <dataType>) # self._consolidator = self.resolve_consolidator(self._symbol, timedelta(days=1))

![image 256](<Quantconnect-Writing-Algorithms-Python_part2_images/imageFile256.png>)

PY

self._consolidator = DynamicDataConsolidator(timedelta(days=1), start_time=timedelta(hours=9, minutes=30))

![image 257](<Quantconnect-Writing-Algorithms-Python_part2_images/imageFile257.png>)

PY

self._consolidator = self.resolve_consolidator(self._symbol, Resolution.DAILY)

![image 258](<Quantconnect-Writing-Algorithms-Python_part2_images/imageFile258.png>)

PY

self._consolidator.data_consolidated += self._consolidation_handler

LEAN passes consolidated bars to the consolidator event handler in your algorithm. The most common error when creating consolidators is to put parenthesis () at the end of your method name when setting the event handler of the consolidator. If you use parenthesis, the method executes and the result is passed as the event handler instead of the method itself. Remember to pass the name of your method to the event system. Specifically, it should be self._consolidation_handler , not self._consolidation_handler() .

- 3. Define the consolidation handler.

The consolidation event handler receives bars when the consolidated bar closes based on the exchange time zone .

- 4. Update the consolidator.

You can automatically or manually update the consolidator.

Automatic Updates

To automatically update a consolidator with data from the data subscription, call the add_consolidator method of the Subscription Manager.

Manual Updates

Manual updates let you control when the consolidator updates and what data you use to update it. If you need to warm up a consolidator with data outside of the warm-up period , you can manually update the consolidator. To manually update a consolidator, call its update method with a <dataType> object. You can update the consolidator with data from the Slice object in the on_data method or with data from a history request .

- 5. If you create consolidators for securities in a dynamic universe and register them for automatic updates, remove the consolidator when the security leaves the universe.


![image 259](<Quantconnect-Writing-Algorithms-Python_part2_images/imageFile259.png>)

PY

def _consolidation_handler(self, sender: object, consolidated_bar: <dataType>) -> None: pass

![image 260](<Quantconnect-Writing-Algorithms-Python_part2_images/imageFile260.png>)

PY

self.subscription_manager.add_consolidator(self._symbol, self._consolidator)

![image 261](<Quantconnect-Writing-Algorithms-Python_part2_images/imageFile261.png>)

self._consolidator.update(data)

self.subscription_manager.remove_consolidator(self._symbol, self._consolidator)

For an example of removing consolidators from universe subscriptions, see the GasAndCrudeOilEnergyCorrelationAlpha in the LEAN GitHub repository.

You can also use the Consolidate helper method to create period consolidators and register them for automatic updates. With just one line of code, you can create data in any time period based on a timedelta or Resolution object:

timedelta Periods

![image 263](<Quantconnect-Writing-Algorithms-Python_part2_images/imageFile263.png>)

PY

self._consolidator = self.consolidate(self._symbol, timedelta(days=1), self._consolidation_handler)

Resolution Periods

def _consolidation_handler(self, consolidated_bar: <dataType>) -> None: pass

### Reset Consolidators

To reset a consolidator, call its reset method.

![image 266](<Quantconnect-Writing-Algorithms-Python_part2_images/imageFile266.png>)

PY

self._consolidator.reset()

If you are live trading Equities or backtesting Equities without the adjusted data normalization mode , reset your consolidators when splits and dividends occur. When a split or dividend occurs while the consolidator is in the process of building a bar, the open, high, and low may reflect prices from before the split or dividend. To avoid issues, call the consolidator's reset method and then warm it up with SCALED_RAW data from a history request .

![image 267](<Quantconnect-Writing-Algorithms-Python_part2_images/imageFile267.png>)

PY

def on_data(self, data: Slice): # When a split or dividend occurs... if (data.splits.contains_key(self._symbol) and data.splits[self._symbol].type == SplitType.SPLIT_OCCURRED or

data.dividends.contains_key(self._symbol)): # If the consolidator is working on a bar... if self._consolidator.working_data:

# Get adjusted prices for the time period of the working bar. history = self.history[TradeBar](self._symbol, self._consolidator.working_data.time, self.time,

data_normalization_mode=DataNormalizationMode.SCALED_RAW) # Reset the consolidator. self._consolidator.reset() # Warm-up the consolidator with the adjusted price data. for bar in history:

self._consolidator.update(bar)

### Examples

The following examples demonstrate common practices for implementing time-period consolidators.

- Example 1: RSI-MACD Trading


The following algorithm utilizes a 5-minute trade bar consolidator to feed the smoothed trade bar to an RSI indicator and a 15-minute quote bar consolidator to feed the smoothed quote bar to a MACD indicator, generating trade entry signals during an uptrend and oversold conditions while exiting when the trend turns down.

![image 268](<Quantconnect-Writing-Algorithms-Python_part2_images/imageFile268.png>)

PY

class UniverseSettingsAlgorithm(QCAlgorithm): # Create RSI and MACD indicators for trade signal generation. _rsi = RelativeStrengthIndex(14) _macd = MovingAverageConvergenceDivergence(12, 26, 9)

def initialize(self) -> None:

self.set_start_date(2024, 9, 1) self.set_end_date(2024, 12, 31)

# Request SPY data to feed to indicators and trade. self.spy = self.add_equity("SPY").symbol

# Create a 5-minute trade bar consolidator to feed the smoothed bar to the RSI indicator for the price action entry signal. rsi_consolidator = TradeBarConsolidator(timedelta(minutes=5)) # Create a 15-minute quote bar consolidator to feed the smoothed bar to the MACD consolidator for trend entry/exit signal. macd_consolidator = QuoteBarConsolidator(timedelta(minutes=15))

# Subscribe the consolidators to SPY data to automatically update the indicators. self.register_indicator(self.spy, self._rsi, rsi_consolidator) self.register_indicator(self.spy, self._macd, macd_consolidator)

# Warm up the indicators by updating the consolidators with historical data. trade_bars = self.history[TradeBar](self.spy, 5 * self._rsi.warm_up_period, Resolution.MINUTE) for bar in trade_bars:

rsi_consolidator.update(bar) quote_bars = self.history[QuoteBar](self.spy, 15 * self._macd.warm_up_period, Resolution.MINUTE) for bar in quote_bars:

macd_consolidator.update(bar)

def on_data(self, slice: Slice) -> None: # Entry rule: MACD histogram is positive (positive trend) and RSI is below 30 (oversold) to maximize return. if self._rsi.current.value < 30 and self._macd.histogram.current.value > 0 and not self.portfolio[self.spy].is_long:

self.set_holdings(self.spy, 0.5) # Exit rule: MACD histogram is below 0 (negative trend) elif self._macd.histogram.current.value < 0 and self.portfolio[self.spy].is_long:

self.liquidate(self.spy)

- Example 2: Create Daily QuoteBar Objects That Start at 5 PM


The following algorithm consolidates minute resolution Forex data into daily QuoteBar objects that open and close at 5 PM Eastern Time.

![image 269](<Quantconnect-Writing-Algorithms-Python_part2_images/imageFile269.png>)

PY

class CustomStartTimeConsolidatorExampleAlgorithm(QCAlgorithm):

def initialize(self) -> None: self.set_start_date(2024, 9, 1) self.set_end_date(2024, 12, 31) # Add the USDJPY Forex pair. self._pair = self.add_forex("USDJPY") # Create an EMA indicator for the pair. self._pair.ema = ExponentialMovingAverage(10) # Create a QuoteBar consolidator with a custom start time. consolidator = QuoteBarConsolidator(timedelta(1), timedelta(hours=17)) # Attach a consolidation handler that will receive the consolidated bars. consolidator.data_consolidated += self._consolidation_handler # Subscribe the consolidator for automatic updates with the prices of the pair. self.subscription_manager.add_consolidator(self._pair.symbol, consolidator) # Register the indicator for automatic updates with the consolidated bars. self.register_indicator(self._pair.symbol, self._pair.ema, consolidator) # Warm up the consolidator and indicator. history = self.history[QuoteBar](self._pair.symbol, 29000, Resolution.MINUTE) for bar in history:

consolidator.update(bar)

def _consolidation_handler(self, sender: object, consolidated_bar: QuoteBar) -> None: # Wait until the indicator is ready and the algorithm is running. if not self._pair.ema.is_ready or self.is_warming_up:

return # Plot the closing price and the EMA. self.plot(consolidated_bar.symbol.value, 'Close', consolidated_bar.close) self.plot(consolidated_bar.symbol.value, 'EMA', self._pair.ema.current.value)

if not self._pair.holdings.is_long and consolidated_bar.close > self._pair.ema.current.value: self.set_holdings(self._pair.symbol, 1) if consolidated_bar.close < self._pair.ema.current.value: self.liquidate()

Other Examples

For more examples, see the following algorithms:

Demonstration Algorithms BasicTemplateOptionsConsolidationAlgorithm.py Python DataConsolidationAlgorithm.py Python MultipleSymbolConsolidationAlgorithm.py Python

Consolidating Data > Consolidator Types > Calendar Consolidators

# Consolidator Types

## Calendar Consolidators

### Introduction

Calendar consolidators aggregate data based on specific periods of time like a weekly basis, quarterly basis, or any schedule with specific start and end times. In contrast to time period consolidators, the bars that calendar consolidators produce always end on the same schedule, regardless of the first data point the consolidator receives.

### Consolidate Trade Bars

TradeBar consolidators aggregate TradeBar objects into TradeBar objects of the same size or larger. Follow these steps to create and manage a TradeBar consolidator based on custom start and end periods:

- 1. Create the consolidator.


To set the time period for the consolidator, you can use the built-in CalendarInfo objects or create your own. The following list describes each technique:

Standard Periods

The following table describes the helper methods that the Calendar class provides to create the built-in CalendarInfo objects:

|Method|Descriptioni i|
|---|---|
|Calendar.WEEKLY|Computes the start of week (previous Monday) of the given date/time|
|Calendar.MONTHLY|Computes the start of month (1st of the current month) of the given date/time|
|Calendar.QUARTERLY|Computes the start of quarter (1st of the starting month of the current quarter) of the given date/time|
|Calendar.YEARLY|Computes the start of year (1st of the current year) of the given date/time|


![image 270](<Quantconnect-Writing-Algorithms-Python_part2_images/imageFile270.png>)

PY

self._consolidator = TradeBarConsolidator(Calendar.WEEKLY) # Alias: # self._consolidator = self.create_consolidator(Calendar.WEEKLY, TradeBar)

Custom Periods

If you need something more specific than the preceding time periods, define a method to set the start time and period of the consolidated bars. The method should receive a datetime object that's based in the exchange time zone and should return a CalendarInfo object, which contains the start time of the bar in the exchange time zone and the duration of the consolidation period. The following example demonstrates how to create a custom consolidator for weekly bars:

self._consolidator = TradeBarConsolidator(self._consolidation_period)

- 2. Add an event handler to the consolidator.

LEAN passes consolidated bars to the consolidator event handler in your algorithm. The most common error when creating consolidators is to put parenthesis () at the end of your method name when setting the event handler of the consolidator. If you use parenthesis, the method executes and the result is passed as the event handler instead of the method itself. Remember to pass the name of your method to the event system. Specifically, it should be self._consolidation_handler , not self._consolidation_handler() .

- 3. Define the consolidation handler.

If you use a custom consolidation period method, LEAN passes the consolidated bar to the consolidation handler when the consolidation period ends. The time and end_time properties of the consolidated bar reflect the exchange time zone, but the time property of the algorithm still reflects the algorithm time zone .

- 4. Update the consolidator.


![image 272](<Quantconnect-Writing-Algorithms-Python_part2_images/imageFile272.png>)

PY

self._consolidator.data_consolidated += self._consolidation_handler

![image 273](<Quantconnect-Writing-Algorithms-Python_part2_images/imageFile273.png>)

PY

def _consolidation_handler(self, sender: object, consolidated_bar: TradeBar) -> None: pass

You can automatically or manually update the consolidator.

Automatic Updates

To automatically update a consolidator with data from the security subscription, call the add_consolidator method of the Subscription Manager.

![image 274](<Quantconnect-Writing-Algorithms-Python_part2_images/imageFile274.png>)

PY

self.subscription_manager.add_consolidator(self._symbol, self._consolidator)

Manual Updates

Manual updates let you control when the consolidator updates and what data you use to update it. If you need to warm up a consolidator with data outside of the warm-up period , you can manually update the consolidator. To manually update a consolidator, call its update method with a TradeBar object. You can update the consolidator with data from the Slice object in the on_data method or with data from a history request .

![image 275](<Quantconnect-Writing-Algorithms-Python_part2_images/imageFile275.png>)

PY

- # Example 1: Update the consolidator with data from the Slice object def on_data(self, slice: Slice) -> None:

trade_bar = slice.bars[self._symbol] self._consolidator.update(trade_bar)

- # Example 2: Update the consolidator with data from a history request history = self.history[TradeBar](self._symbol, 30, Resolution.MINUTE) for trade_bar in history:


self._consolidator.update(trade_bar)

self._consolidator = self.consolidate(self._symbol, Calendar.WEEKLY, self._consolidation_handler)

If you use the consolidate helper method, the consolidation handler doesn't receive an object argument.

![image 278](<Quantconnect-Writing-Algorithms-Python_part2_images/imageFile278.png>)

PY

def _consolidation_handler(self, consolidated_bar: TradeBar) -> None: pass

### Consolidate Quote Bars

QuoteBar consolidators aggregate QuoteBar objects into QuoteBar objects of the same size or larger. Follow these steps to create and manage a QuoteBar consolidator based on custom start and end periods:

- 1. Create the consolidator.


To set the time period for the consolidator, you can use the built-in CalendarInfo objects or create your own. The following list describes each technique:

Standard Periods

The following table describes the helper methods that the Calendar class provides to create the built-in CalendarInfo objects:

|Method|Descriptioni i|
|---|---|
|Calendar.WEEKLY|Computes the start of week (previous Monday) of the given date/time|
|Calendar.MONTHLY|Computes the start of month (1st of the current month) of the given date/time|
|Calendar.QUARTERLY|Computes the start of quarter (1st of the starting month of the current quarter) of the given date/time|
|Calendar.YEARLY|Computes the start of year (1st of the current year) of the given date/time|


![image 279](<Quantconnect-Writing-Algorithms-Python_part2_images/imageFile279.png>)

PY

self._consolidator = QuoteBarConsolidator(Calendar.WEEKLY) # Alias: # self._consolidator = self.create_consolidator(Calendar.WEEKLY, QuoteBar)

self._consolidator = QuoteBarConsolidator(self._consolidation_period)

- 2. Add an event handler to the consolidator.

LEAN passes consolidated bars to the consolidator event handler in your algorithm. The most common error when creating consolidators is to put parenthesis () at the end of your method name when setting the event handler of the consolidator. If you use parenthesis, the method executes and the result is passed as the event handler instead of the method itself. Remember to pass the name of your method to the event system. Specifically, it should be self._consolidation_handler , not self._consolidation_handler() .

- 3. Define the consolidation handler.

If you use a custom consolidation period method, LEAN passes the consolidated bar to the consolidation handler when the consolidation period ends. The time and end_time properties of the consolidated bar reflect the exchange time zone, but the time property of the algorithm still reflects the algorithm time zone .

- 4. Update the consolidator.


![image 281](<Quantconnect-Writing-Algorithms-Python_part2_images/imageFile281.png>)

PY

self._consolidator.data_consolidated += self._consolidation_handler

![image 282](<Quantconnect-Writing-Algorithms-Python_part2_images/imageFile282.png>)

PY

def _consolidation_handler(self, sender: object, consolidated_bar: QuoteBar) -> None: pass

You can automatically or manually update the consolidator.

Automatic Updates

To automatically update a consolidator with data from the security subscription, call the add_consolidator method of the Subscription Manager.

![image 283](<Quantconnect-Writing-Algorithms-Python_part2_images/imageFile283.png>)

PY

self.subscription_manager.add_consolidator(self._symbol, self._consolidator)

Manual Updates

Manual updates let you control when the consolidator updates and what data you use to update it. If you need to warm up a consolidator with data outside of the warm-up period , you can manually update the consolidator. To manually update a consolidator, call its update method with a QuoteBar object. You can update the consolidator with data from the Slice object in the on_data method or with data from a history request .

![image 284](<Quantconnect-Writing-Algorithms-Python_part2_images/imageFile284.png>)

PY

# Example 1: Update the consolidator with data from the Slice object def on_data(self, slice: Slice) -> None:

quote_bar = slice.quote_bars[self._symbol] self._consolidator.update(quote_bar)

# Example 2: Update the consolidator with data from a history request history = self.history[QuoteBar](self._symbol, 30, Resolution.MINUTE) for quote_bar in history:

self._consolidator.update(quote_bar)

self._consolidator = self.consolidate(self._symbol, Calendar.WEEKLY, TickType.QUOTE, self._consolidation_handler)

If you use the consolidate helper method, the consolidation handler doesn't receive an object argument.

![image 287](<Quantconnect-Writing-Algorithms-Python_part2_images/imageFile287.png>)

PY

def _consolidation_handler(self, consolidated_bar: QuoteBar) -> None: pass

|Calendar.YEARLY|Computes the start of year (1st of the current year) of the given date/time|

### Consolidate Quote Ticks

Tick quote bar consolidators aggregate Tick objects that represent quotes into QuoteBar objects. Follow these steps to create and manage a Tick quote bar consolidator based on custom start and end periods:

1. Create the consolidator.

To set the time period for the consolidator, you can use the built-in CalendarInfo objects or create your own. The following list describes each technique:

Standard Periods

The following table describes the helper methods that the Calendar class provides to create the built-in CalendarInfo objects:

|Method|Descriptioni i|
|---|---|
|Calendar.WEEKLY|Computes the start of week (previous Monday) of the given date/time|
|Calendar.MONTHLY|Computes the start of month (1st of the current month) of the given date/time|
|Calendar.QUARTERLY|Computes the start of quarter (1st of the starting month of the current quarter) of the given date/time|
|Calendar.YEARLY|Computes the start of year (1st of the current year) of the given date/time|


![image 297](<Quantconnect-Writing-Algorithms-Python_part2_images/imageFile297.png>)

PY

self._consolidator = TickQuoteBarConsolidator(Calendar.WEEKLY) # Alias: # self._consolidator = self.create_consolidator(Calendar.WEEKLY, Tick, TickType.QUOTE)

Custom Periods

If you need something more specific than the preceding time periods, define a method to set the start time and period of the consolidated bars. The method should receive a datetime object that's based in the exchange time zone and should return a CalendarInfo object, which contains the start time of the bar in the exchange time zone and the duration of the consolidation period. The following example demonstrates how to create a custom consolidator for weekly bars:

self._consolidator = TickQuoteBarConsolidator(self._consolidation_period)

2. Add an event handler to the consolidator.

![image 299](<Quantconnect-Writing-Algorithms-Python_part2_images/imageFile299.png>)

self._consolidator.data_consolidated += self._consolidation_handler

LEAN passes consolidated bars to the consolidator event handler in your algorithm. The most common error when creating consolidators is to put parenthesis () at the end of your method name when setting the event handler of the consolidator. If you use parenthesis, the method executes and the result is passed as the event handler instead of the method itself. Remember to pass the name of your method to the event system. Specifically, it should be self._consolidation_handler , not self._consolidation_handler() .

3. Define the consolidation handler.

![image 300](<Quantconnect-Writing-Algorithms-Python_part2_images/imageFile300.png>)

PY

If you use a custom consolidation period method, LEAN passes the consolidated bar to the consolidation handler when the consolidation period ends. The time and end_time properties of the consolidated bar reflect the exchange time zone, but the time property of the algorithm still reflects the algorithm time zone .

You can automatically or manually update the consolidator.

Automatic Updates

To automatically update a consolidator with data from the security subscription, call the add_consolidator method of the Subscription Manager.

### Consolidate Other Data

<dataType> consolidators aggregate various types of data objects into <dataType> objects of the same size or larger. If you consolidate custom data or alternative datasets, check the definition of the data class to see its data type. Follow these steps to create and manage a <dataType> consolidator based on custom start and end periods:

- 1. Create the consolidator.


To set the time period for the consolidator, you can use the built-in CalendarInfo objects or create your own. The following list describes each technique:

Standard Periods

The following table describes the helper methods that the Calendar class provides to create the built-in CalendarInfo objects:

|Method|Descriptioni i|
|---|---|
|Calendar.WEEKLY|Computes the start of week (previous Monday) of the given date/time|
|Calendar.MONTHLY|Computes the start of month (1st of the current month) of the given date/time|
|Calendar.QUARTERLY|Computes the start of quarter (1st of the starting month of the current quarter) of the given date/time|
|Calendar.YEARLY|Computes the start of year (1st of the current year) of the given date/time|


![image 306](<Quantconnect-Writing-Algorithms-Python_part2_images/imageFile306.png>)

PY

self._consolidator = DynamicDataConsolidator(Calendar.WEEKLY) # Alias: # self._consolidator = self.create_consolidator(Calendar.WEEKLY, <dataType>)

Custom Periods

If you need something more specific than the preceding time periods, define a method to set the start time and period of the consolidated bars. The method should receive a datetime object that's based in the exchange time zone and should return a CalendarInfo object, which contains the start time of the bar in the exchange time zone and the duration of the consolidation period. The following example demonstrates how to create a custom consolidator for weekly bars:

self._consolidator = DynamicDataConsolidator(self._consolidation_period)

- 2. Add an event handler to the consolidator.

LEAN passes consolidated bars to the consolidator event handler in your algorithm. The most common error when creating consolidators is to put parenthesis () at the end of your method name when setting the event handler of the consolidator. If you use parenthesis, the method executes and the result is passed as the event handler instead of the method itself. Remember to pass the name of your method to the event system. Specifically, it should be self._consolidation_handler , not self._consolidation_handler() .

- 3. Define the consolidation handler.

If you use a custom consolidation period method, LEAN passes the consolidated bar to the consolidation handler when the consolidation period ends. The time and end_time properties of the consolidated bar reflect the exchange time zone, but the time property of the algorithm still reflects the algorithm time zone .

- 4. Update the consolidator.

You can automatically or manually update the consolidator.

Automatic Updates

To automatically update a consolidator with data from the data subscription, call the add_consolidator method of the Subscription Manager.

Manual Updates

Manual updates let you control when the consolidator updates and what data you use to update it. If you need to warm up a consolidator with data outside of the warm-up period , you can manually update the consolidator. To manually update a consolidator, call its update method with a <dataType> object. You can update the consolidator with data from the Slice object in the on_data method or with data from a history request .

- 5. If you create consolidators for securities in a dynamic universe and register them for automatic updates, remove the consolidator when the

# Example 1: Update the consolidator with data from the Slice object def on_data(self, slice: Slice) -> None:

if self._symbol in slice: self._consolidator.update(slice[self._symbol])

security leaves the universe.

self.subscription_manager.remove_consolidator(self._symbol, self._consolidator)

If you have a dynamic universe and don't remove consolidators, they compound internally, causing your algorithm to slow down and eventually die once it runs out of RAM. For an example of removing consolidators from universe subscriptions, see the GasAndCrudeOilEnergyCorrelationAlpha in the LEAN GitHub repository.

You can also use the consolidate helper method to create calendar consolidators and register them for automatic updates.

![image 313](<Quantconnect-Writing-Algorithms-Python_part2_images/imageFile313.png>)

PY

self._consolidator = self.consolidate(self._symbol, Calendar.WEEKLY, self._consolidation_handler)

If you use the consolidate helper method, the consolidation handler doesn't receive an object argument.

![image 314](<Quantconnect-Writing-Algorithms-Python_part2_images/imageFile314.png>)

PY

def _consolidation_handler(self, consolidated_bar: <dataType>) -> None: pass

### Reset Consolidators

To reset a consolidator, call its reset method.

![image 315](<Quantconnect-Writing-Algorithms-Python_part2_images/imageFile315.png>)

PY

self._consolidator.reset()

If you are live trading Equities or backtesting Equities without the adjusted data normalization mode , reset your consolidators when splits and dividends occur. When a split or dividend occurs while the consolidator is in the process of building a bar, the open, high, and low may reflect prices from before the split or dividend. To avoid issues, call the consolidator's reset method and then warm it up with SCALED_RAW data from a history request .

![image 316](<Quantconnect-Writing-Algorithms-Python_part2_images/imageFile316.png>)

PY

def on_data(self, data: Slice): # When a split or dividend occurs... if (data.splits.contains_key(self._symbol) and data.splits[self._symbol].type == SplitType.SPLIT_OCCURRED or

data.dividends.contains_key(self._symbol)): # If the consolidator is working on a bar... if self._consolidator.working_data:

# Get adjusted prices for the time period of the working bar. history = self.history[TradeBar](self._symbol, self._consolidator.working_data.time, self.time,

data_normalization_mode=DataNormalizationMode.SCALED_RAW) # Reset the consolidator. self._consolidator.reset() # Warm-up the consolidator with the adjusted price data. for bar in history:

self._consolidator.update(bar)

### Examples

The following examples demonstrate some common practices for calendar consolidators.

- Example 1: Daily Futures Bars


Daily bars from the CME and Yahoo Finance represent the price action from 5 PM Central Time (CT) to 4 PM CT on the following day. In contrast, the regular daily resolution bars on QuantConnect represent the price action from 12 AM Eastern Time (ET) to 12 AM ET the following day. To create daily bars that represent the same period of time as the daily bars from the CME, the following algorithm consolidates minute bars (during regular and extended market hours) into daily bars that span from 5 PM CT to 4 PM CT on the following day. The algorithm then uses these consolidated daily bars to place take profit and stop loss orders as a function of the daily Average True Range .

![image 317](<Quantconnect-Writing-Algorithms-Python_part2_images/imageFile317.png>)

PY

class CalendarConsolidatorExampleAlgorithm(QCAlgorithm):

def initialize(self) -> None: self.set_start_date(2024, 9, 1) self.set_end_date(2024, 12, 31) # Create an ATR indicator to set take profit and stop loss levels. self._atr = AverageTrueRange(10) # Create a day tracker to avoid overtrading. self._day = -1 # Add ES Futures data and configure the continuous contract settings # so you can update the ATR with a smooth feed of prices. self._future = self.add_future(

Futures.Indices.SP_500_E_MINI, extended_market_hours=True, data_normalization_mode=DataNormalizationMode.BACKWARDS_RATIO, data_mapping_mode=DataMappingMode.OPEN_INTEREST, contract_depth_offset=0

) # Create a calendar consolidator to generate daily ES Futures bars # since market starts at 6pm EST to 5pm EST next day. consolidator = TradeBarConsolidator(self._future_trading_hour) consolidator.data_consolidated += self._on_consolidated # Warm up the consolidator and ATR indicator. history = self.history[TradeBar](self._future.symbol, 14500, Resolution.MINUTE) for bar in history:

consolidator.update(bar) # Subscribe the consolidator for automatic updates with ES data. self.subscription_manager.add_consolidator(self._future.symbol, consolidator)

def _future_trading_hour(self, dt: datetime) -> CalendarInfo: # Set the open time of the bar to be 6 PM EST. start = dt.replace(hour=0, minute=0, second=0, microsecond=0) if dt.hour < 18:

start -= timedelta(hours=6) else:

start += timedelta(hours=18) # Set the close time of the bar to be 5 PM EST the next day, which is 23 hours later. return CalendarInfo(start, timedelta(hours=23))

def _on_consolidated(self, sender: object, bar: TradeBar) -> None: # Update the ATR indicator since we want the daily ATR to set the stop price. self._atr.update(bar) # Set the daily take profit and stop price levels as a function of the previous # close price and the ATR. self._take_profit_price = bar.close + self._atr.current.value * 2 self._stop_loss_price = bar.close - self._atr.current.value * 2

def on_data(self, slice: Slice) -> None: # Liquidate the current contract in the continuous contract series # when it's about to expire. changed_event = slice.symbol_changed_events.get(self._future.symbol) if changed_event:

self.liquidate(changed_event.old_symbol)

# If we're not invested, buy the current contract in the continuous contract series. if (not self.portfolio.invested and

self.securities[self._future.mapped].price and self._day != slice.time.day): self.market_order(self._future.mapped, 1)

# If we're already invested, check if we've hit the TP/SL level. elif self.portfolio.invested and self._future.symbol in slice.bars:

current_price = slice.bars[self._future.symbol].close # Check if the current price is above profit taker or below stop loss. if current_price > self._take_profit_price or current_price < self._stop_loss_price:

# Exit the position. self.liquidate()

# Update the daily tracker so we only update our position once per day. self._day = slice.time.day

- Example 2: Create Daily QuoteBar Objects That Start at 5 PM


The following algorithm consolidates minute resolution Forex data into daily QuoteBar objects that open and close at 5 PM Eastern Time.

![image 318](<Quantconnect-Writing-Algorithms-Python_part2_images/imageFile318.png>)

PY

class CalendarConsolidatorExampleAlgorithm(QCAlgorithm):

def initialize(self) -> None: self.set_start_date(2024, 9, 1) self.set_end_date(2024, 12, 31) # Add the USDJPY Forex pair. self._pair = self.add_forex("USDJPY") # Create an EMA indicator for the pair. self._pair.ema = ExponentialMovingAverage(10) # Create a QuoteBar consolidator with a custom consolidation period. consolidator = QuoteBarConsolidator(self._daily_forex_consolidation_period) # Attach a consolidation handler that will receive the consolidated bars. consolidator.data_consolidated += self._consolidation_handler # Subscribe the consolidator for automatic updates with the prices of the pair. self.subscription_manager.add_consolidator(self._pair.symbol, consolidator) # Register the indicator for automatic updates with the consolidated bars. self.register_indicator(self._pair.symbol, self._pair.ema, consolidator) # Warm up the consolidator and indicator. history = self.history[QuoteBar](self._pair.symbol, 29000, Resolution.MINUTE) for bar in history:

consolidator.update(bar)

# Define the consolidation period. def _daily_forex_consolidation_period(self, dt: datetime) -> CalendarInfo:

# Set the start of the bar to be 5 PM ET. start = dt.replace(hour=0, minute=0, second=0, microsecond=0) if dt.hour < 17:

start -= timedelta(hours=7) else:

start += timedelta(hours=17) # Set the end of the bar to be 5 PM ET the next day. return CalendarInfo(start, timedelta(1))

def _consolidation_handler(self, sender: object, consolidated_bar: QuoteBar) -> None: # Wait until the indicator is ready and the algorithm is running. if not self._pair.ema.is_ready or self.is_warming_up:

return # Plot the closing price and the EMA. self.plot(str(consolidated_bar.symbol), 'Close', consolidated_bar.close) self.plot(str(consolidated_bar.symbol), 'EMA', self._pair.ema.current.value)

For a version of this algorithm with Time Period Consolidators, see Time Period Consolidators > Examples .

- Example 3: Monthly Candlesticks


The following algorithm consolidates minute-resolution data into monthly TradeBar objects and then plots them as candlesticks .

![image 319](<Quantconnect-Writing-Algorithms-Python_part2_images/imageFile319.png>)

PY

from dateutil.relativedelta import relativedelta

class CalendarConsolidatorExampleAlgorithm(QCAlgorithm):

def initialize(self) -> None: self.set_start_date(2024, 9, 1) self.set_end_date(2024, 12, 31) # Add an asset. symbol = self.add_equity("SPY").symbol # Create a consolidator that produces monthly TradeBar objects. # This is the stardard way: #consolidator = TradeBarConsolidator(Calendar.MONTHLY) # However, you can acomplish the same thing with a custom period: consolidator = TradeBarConsolidator(self._custom_monthly) # Add a consolidation handler that plots the monthly bars as candlesticks. consolidator.data_consolidated += lambda _, bar: self.plot(

"Monthly Candlestick", symbol.value, bar.open, bar.high, bar.low, bar.close

) # Register the consolidator for automatic updates. self.subscription_manager.add_consolidator(symbol, consolidator)

def _custom_monthly(self, dt: datetime) -> CalendarInfo: # Set the start time to the beginning of the month. start = dt.replace(day=1).date() # Set the end time to the beginning of next month. end = start + relativedelta(months=1) return CalendarInfo(start, end - start)

Other Examples

For more examples, see the following algorithms:

Demonstration Algorithms DataConsolidationAlgorithm.py Python

Consolidating Data > Consolidator Types > Count Consolidators

# Consolidator Types

## Count Consolidators

### Introduction

Count consolidators aggregate data based on a number of bars or ticks. This type of consolidator aggregates n samples together, regardless of the time period the samples cover. Managing count consolidators is similiar to managing time period consolidators , except you create count consolidators with an integer argument instead of a time-based argument.

### Consolidate Trade Bars

TradeBar consolidators aggregate TradeBar objects into TradeBar objects of the same size or larger. Follow these steps to create and manage a TradeBar consolidator based on a number of samples:

- 1. Create the consolidator.

To create a count consolidator, pass the number of samples to the consolidator constructor.

- 2. Add an event handler to the consolidator.

LEAN passes consolidated bars to the consolidator event handler in your algorithm. The most common error when creating consolidators is to put parenthesis () at the end of your method name when setting the event handler of the consolidator. If you use parenthesis, the method executes and the result is passed as the event handler instead of the method itself. Remember to pass the name of your method to the event system. Specifically, it should be self._consolidation_handler , not self._consolidation_handler() .

- 3. Define the consolidation handler.

When the consolidator receives the n -th data point, it passes the consolidated bar to the event handler.

- 4. Update the consolidator.


![image 320](<Quantconnect-Writing-Algorithms-Python_part2_images/imageFile320.png>)

PY

self._consolidator = TradeBarConsolidator(10)

![image 321](<Quantconnect-Writing-Algorithms-Python_part2_images/imageFile321.png>)

PY

self._consolidator.data_consolidated += self._consolidation_handler

![image 322](<Quantconnect-Writing-Algorithms-Python_part2_images/imageFile322.png>)

PY

def _consolidation_handler(self, sender: object, consolidated_bar: TradeBar) -> None: pass

You can automatically or manually update the consolidator.

Automatic Updates

To automatically update a consolidator with data from the security subscription, call the add_consolidator method of the Subscription Manager.

![image 323](<Quantconnect-Writing-Algorithms-Python_part2_images/imageFile323.png>)

PY

self.subscription_manager.add_consolidator(self._symbol, self._consolidator)

Manual Updates

Manual updates let you control when the consolidator updates and what data you use to update it. If you need to warm up a consolidator with data outside of the warm-up period , you can manually update the consolidator. To manually update a consolidator, call its update method with a TradeBar object. You can update the consolidator with data from the Slice object in the on_data method or with data from a history request .

![image 324](<Quantconnect-Writing-Algorithms-Python_part2_images/imageFile324.png>)

PY

- # Example 1: Update the consolidator with data from the Slice object def on_data(self, slice: Slice) -> None:

trade_bar = slice.bars[self._symbol] self._consolidator.update(trade_bar)

- # Example 2: Update the consolidator with data from a history request history = self.history[TradeBar](self._symbol, 30, Resolution.MINUTE) for trade_bar in history:


self._consolidator.update(trade_bar)

- 5. If you create consolidators for securities in a dynamic universe and register them for automatic updates, remove the consolidator when the security leaves the universe.


![image 325](<Quantconnect-Writing-Algorithms-Python_part2_images/imageFile325.png>)

PY

self.subscription_manager.remove_consolidator(self._symbol, self._consolidator)

If you have a dynamic universe and don't remove consolidators, they compound internally, causing your algorithm to slow down and eventually die once it runs out of RAM. For an example of removing consolidators from universe subscriptions, see the GasAndCrudeOilEnergyCorrelationAlpha in the LEAN GitHub repository.

### Consolidate Quote Bars

QuoteBar consolidators aggregate QuoteBar objects into QuoteBar objects of the same size or larger. Follow these steps to create and manage a QuoteBar consolidator based on a number of samples:

- 1. Create the consolidator.

To create a count consolidator, pass the number of samples to the consolidator constructor.

- 2. Add an event handler to the consolidator.

LEAN passes consolidated bars to the consolidator event handler in your algorithm. The most common error when creating consolidators is to put parenthesis () at the end of your method name when setting the event handler of the consolidator. If you use parenthesis, the method executes and the result is passed as the event handler instead of the method itself. Remember to pass the name of your method to the event system. Specifically, it should be self._consolidation_handler , not self._consolidation_handler() .

- 3. Define the consolidation handler.

When the consolidator receives the n -th data point, it passes the consolidated bar to the event handler.

- 4. Update the consolidator.


![image 326](<Quantconnect-Writing-Algorithms-Python_part2_images/imageFile326.png>)

PY

self._consolidator = QuoteBarConsolidator(10)

![image 327](<Quantconnect-Writing-Algorithms-Python_part2_images/imageFile327.png>)

PY

self._consolidator.data_consolidated += self._consolidation_handler

![image 328](<Quantconnect-Writing-Algorithms-Python_part2_images/imageFile328.png>)

PY

def _consolidation_handler(self, sender: object, consolidated_bar: QuoteBar) -> None: pass

You can automatically or manually update the consolidator.

Automatic Updates

To automatically update a consolidator with data from the security subscription, call the add_consolidator method of the Subscription Manager.

![image 329](<Quantconnect-Writing-Algorithms-Python_part2_images/imageFile329.png>)

PY

self.subscription_manager.add_consolidator(self._symbol, self._consolidator)

Manual Updates

Manual updates let you control when the consolidator updates and what data you use to update it. If you need to warm up a consolidator with data outside of the warm-up period , you can manually update the consolidator. To manually update a consolidator, call its update method with a QuoteBar object. You can update the consolidator with data from the Slice object in the on_data method or with data from a history request .

![image 330](<Quantconnect-Writing-Algorithms-Python_part2_images/imageFile330.png>)

PY

- # Example 1: Update the consolidator with data from the Slice object def on_data(self, slice: Slice) -> None:

quote_bar = slice.quote_bars[self._symbol] self._consolidator.update(quote_bar)

- # Example 2: Update the consolidator with data from a history request history = self.history[QuoteBar](self._symbol, 30, Resolution.MINUTE) for quote_bar in history:


self._consolidator.update(quote_bar)

- 5. If you create consolidators for securities in a dynamic universe and register them for automatic updates, remove the consolidator when the security leaves the universe.


![image 331](<Quantconnect-Writing-Algorithms-Python_part2_images/imageFile331.png>)

PY

self.subscription_manager.remove_consolidator(self._symbol, self._consolidator)

If you have a dynamic universe and don't remove consolidators, they compound internally, causing your algorithm to slow down and eventually die once it runs out of RAM. For an example of removing consolidators from universe subscriptions, see the GasAndCrudeOilEnergyCorrelationAlpha in the LEAN GitHub repository.

### Consolidate Trade Ticks

Tick consolidators aggregate Tick objects into TradeBar objects. Follow these steps to create and manage a Tick consolidator based on a number of samples:

- 1. Create the consolidator.

To create a count consolidator, pass the number of samples to the consolidator constructor.

- 2. Add an event handler to the consolidator.

LEAN passes consolidated bars to the consolidator event handler in your algorithm. The most common error when creating consolidators is to put parenthesis () at the end of your method name when setting the event handler of the consolidator. If you use parenthesis, the method executes and the result is passed as the event handler instead of the method itself. Remember to pass the name of your method to the event system. Specifically, it should be self._consolidation_handler , not self._consolidation_handler() .

- 3. Define the consolidation handler.

When the consolidator receives the n -th data point, it passes the consolidated bar to the event handler.

- 4. Update the consolidator.


![image 332](<Quantconnect-Writing-Algorithms-Python_part2_images/imageFile332.png>)

PY

self._consolidator = TickConsolidator(10)

![image 333](<Quantconnect-Writing-Algorithms-Python_part2_images/imageFile333.png>)

PY

self._consolidator.data_consolidated += self._consolidation_handler

![image 334](<Quantconnect-Writing-Algorithms-Python_part2_images/imageFile334.png>)

PY

def _consolidation_handler(self, sender: object, consolidated_bar: TradeBar) -> None: pass

Automatic Updates

Manual Updates

You can update the consolidator with data from the Slice object in the on_data method or with data from a history request .

executes and the result is passed as the event handler instead of the method itself. Remember to pass the name of your method to the event system. Specifically, it should be self._consolidation_handler , not self._consolidation_handler() .

- 3. Define the consolidation handler.

When the consolidator receives the n -th data point, it passes the consolidated bar to the event handler.

- 4. Update the consolidator.

Automatic Updates
